algorithm_summary: The phenotype algorithm defines Type 2 diabetes cases using three case definitions: 1) ICD-9/10 diabetes codes AND non-insulin diabetes medications, 2) ICD-9/10 diabetes codes AND labs (glucose >200 mg/dl OR HbA1c >6.5%) AND NOT insulin medications or non-insulin DM medications, or 3) Non-insulin DM medications AND labs (glucose >200 mg/dl OR HbA1c >6.5%). Controls are defined as records that do not contain any diabetes ICD codes, insulin medications, non-insulin DM medications, family history keywords, or abnormal glucose/HbA1c values (glucose >=110 mg/dl or HbA1c >=6.0%). Population is restricted to age 18 or greater. The updated version includes both ICD-9 and ICD-10 codes for broader compatibility.
